title: Red Sox win 7th straight game just hours after landing in New York
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox secured their seventh consecutive win, defeating the New York Mets 6-2.
Sonny Gray pitched six innings with one run allowed, notching his 11th win of the season.
Anthony Seigler and Wilyer Abreu each hit two-run homers, boosting Boston's offense.

title: Christian Gonzalez responds to report that he isn’t physical enough for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez refuted claims of insufficient physicality made by reporter Greg Bedard on social media.
Gonzalez registered 69 tackles in 2025, comparable to top physical corners in the NFL.
The Patriots' secondary expects improvements with Gonzalez, adding veterans like Kevin Byard and others for 2026.

title: Here’s who experts have the Red Sox selecting with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Several experts predict Boston Red Sox may draft college right-handed pitcher Taylor Rabe with the No. 20 pick in 2026 MLB Draft.
Mock drafts suggest potential selections include Chase Brunson, outfielder from TCU, and Bo Lowrance, high school third baseman.
Expert predictions also highlight Zion Rose, outfielder from Louisville, and Ace Reese, third baseman from Mississippi State.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Long Walk' adapts Stephen King's novel; streaming on HBO Max.
'Project Hail Mary' with Ryan Gosling streams on Prime Video.
'The Five-Star Weekend', filmed on Nantucket, is now on Peacock.

title: A 4th Red Sox player has been named a 2026 MLB All-Star
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ceddanne Rafaela named to 2026 MLB All-Star team, replacing injured Aaron Judge.
Rafaela posts career-best .283/.330/.438 slash line with eight home runs and 39 RBI in 87 games.
Rafaela commits just one error in 734.1 innings, maintaining strong defensive presence in center field.

title: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Joey Chestnut will compete in the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship on July 11 at The Brook Casino.
Geoffrey Esper, current wing eating record holder, will also compete; Esper ate 281 wings in 10 minutes in 2019.
The contest will be held at The Secret Garden in Seabrook, NH, starting at 5:30 p.m. and is free for ages 18+.

title: France-Morocco was a fitting end to a spectacular World Cup in Boston
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in the final World Cup match in Foxborough, with goals by Mbappé and Dembélé.
Boston successfully hosted the 2026 World Cup, attracting a large crowd and international attention despite logistical challenges.
Boston's World Cup saw strong support from both local and international fans, especially from Scotland and Norway.

title: Gloucester woman accused of plotting to kill childhood friend using WD-40
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Phyllis Palazzola of Gloucester faces charges after allegedly attacking Marianna DiMercurio with a WD-40-soaked rag.
DiMercurio claims Palazzola plotted to kill them both, owing her over $160,000 from a previous loan.
Palazzola was indicted on several charges, including attempted murder and witness intimidation, with arraignment set for July 21.

title: How a Waltham barber became France’s go-to during the World Cup
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Waltham barber Coco Fernandez became the trusted barber for France's national soccer team during the World Cup.
Fernandez first cut Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé's hair after a request at Boston's Four Seasons Hotel.
Fernandez has since worked with both France and Morocco's teams, providing services before key World Cup matches.

title: Red Sox face lengthy travel issues ahead of series vs. Mets
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Red Sox faced a 17-hour delay in Chicago before their game against the Mets due to travel issues.
Boston's flight was delayed by plane problems including a broken tug and a cockpit light issue.
The game start time was adjusted to 7:50 p.m., despite the Red Sox arriving in New York at 5 p.m.
